column1: One row per Goal and Prescription Class. Key = Goal|Class, unique, use it as the Firestore document ID. Every number is a plain number, no text, no ranges inside a cell. Empty means not applicable. RIR is reps left in reserve. Higher RIR is easier and safer. Sets, reps, seconds and rest are all min and max pairs.

column18: progression_rule
column19: coach_note

column18: Add 2.5kg upper body or 5kg lower body once the top set hits reps_max in two sessions in a row. Never add more than 5 percent load in a week.
column19: Heavy load with long rest. Reps stay at 4 or above so the app never prescribes a near maximal single.

column18: Move to a harder version of the exercise rather than adding reps. Once a set passes 8 reps, progress the variation.
column19: No load to add, so difficulty comes from leverage.

column18: Add load first, seconds second. Hold time is capped at 40 seconds.
column19: Short heavy holds. Long holds become endurance work.

column18: Add height, distance or load only while movement speed stays the same. Stop the set the moment speed drops.
column19: Never taken near failure. A failed power rep is a fall.

column18: Do not progress. Keep it as an easy warm up only.
column19: Conditioning competes with heavy lifting recovery, so keep it small.

column18: Double progression. Add reps to reps_max, then add weight and return to reps_min.
column19: Hard sets close to failure drive growth. Compounds keep at least 1 rep in reserve.

column18: Add reps, then slow the lowering phase, then move to a harder variation.
column19: Bodyweight sets need to be taken closer to failure than loaded ones.

column18: Add seconds to 60, then add load or move to a harder position.
column19: Past 60 seconds the limit becomes endurance rather than muscle.

column18: Do not chase growth here. Use it as a primer before heavy sets.
column19: Explosive lifts cannot be pushed near failure safely.

column18: Hold steady. Do not add volume.
column19: Enough conditioning to stay fit, not enough to eat into recovery.

column18: Keep the weight the same and cut rest, or hold the reps and add weight. Never let load fall.
column19: In a calorie deficit, lifting keeps the muscle you already have. Cut volume, never load.

column18: Add reps or shorten rest. Can be run as a circuit.
column19: Keeps heart rate up while still loading the muscle.

column18: Add seconds or reduce rest.
column19: Cheap to recover from and keeps total work high.

column18: Add rounds, not reps per round.
column19: Capping the work interval keeps technique intact as fatigue builds.

column18: Add rounds, then extend the work interval, then cut rest.
column19: Short hard intervals. Effort around 8 out of 10, never all out.

column18: Add reps or a small amount of weight whenever the last set feels easy.
column19: Leaving reps in reserve keeps soreness low and keeps the user turning up.

column18: Add reps, then progress the variation.
column19: The goal is a habit that survives exam period.

column18: Add seconds.
column19: Builds trunk endurance and postural tolerance.

column18: Add reps only once the landing is quiet and controlled.
column19: Teaches force absorption, which protects knees and ankles. Skill first.

column18: Add rounds.
column19: Moderate effort, around 6 out of 10. Builds an aerobic base.
